perturbation.original_text: The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote a portion of shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date, such portion determined at the White Hat Parties sole discretion, for such meeting (i) in favor of some persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, with the specific determination of endorsed directors subject to the White Hat Parties sole discretion, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the White Hat Parties vote 'all' of their shares in favor of the Board's nominees. The modified text states they will vote 'a portion' of their shares, determined at their 'sole discretion,' creating a contradiction. The shift from 'all' to 'a portion' and the addition of 'sole discretion' give the White Hat Parties the freedom to not adhere to the original voting commitment. The clause that defines 'Voting Commitments' in the text, is now ambiguous and open to interpretation. Now instead of voting for 'all', they can choose which 'persons' nominated by board to vote for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote a portion of shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date, such portion determined at the White Hat Parties sole discretion, for such meeting (i) in favor of some persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, with the specific determination of endorsed directors subject to the White Hat Parties sole discretion, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the White Hat Parties shall vote 'all' of their shares for the board's nominees. The changed text modifies this to 'a portion' of shares, determined at their 'sole discretion'. This directly contradicts the initial commitment to vote all shares, creating a strong contradiction. The contradicted text restates the changed text, illustrating the altered obligation.
